description:
Add the one §16 base-doctor check deferred by bl-d024: EffectiveConfig (§4) resolution. Spec bl-2e26 §16, verbatim: 'EffectiveConfig (§4) resolves — flag a trail-layer conflict that yields no satisfiable config.' Per the no-repair-verb rule, the named fix is: 'an unsatisfiable resolved config → hand-edit the named config/balls.toml layer' (NOT a verb — a deliberate human act on the conflicting layer).

WHY DEFERRED: bl-d024 built base doctor (src/doctor.rs::audit) with the other five core-owned checks (stale change worktrees, operating/ resolves, bin/ dangle, protocol drift, circular blockers). This sixth check needs the config-layering subsystem (§4 EffectiveConfig), which does not exist yet — bl-651c was the schema/format only; the config LOADER is a separate, not-yet-filed ball. So this rides that loader: it cannot be implemented until EffectiveConfig resolution exists to call.

SCOPE WHEN PICKED UP: one more check appended to doctor::audit — resolve EffectiveConfig over the trail layers; on an unsatisfiable resolution, push a Finding naming the conflicting config/balls.toml layer path and the hand-edit fix. The audit's shape does not change (it already returns Vec<Finding>); this is purely additive, ~one helper + Finding constructor + tests, 100% coverage. Cleanest is to fold this INTO the config-loader ball's scope rather than land it standalone, so the check ships with the capability it audits.
title: §16 doctor: EffectiveConfig-resolves check (rides config layering)
